steps.title: Set up
steps.content: The boundary line is rx + ty = −36. Use the y-intercept (0, −12) from the graph: r(0) + t(−12) = −36, so −12t = −36, giving t = 3.

steps.title: Solve
steps.content:
Use a second point on the line, (−12, −8): r(−12) + 3(−8) = −36 ⇒ −12r − 24 = −36 ⇒ −12r = −12 ⇒ r = 1.
So r + t = 1 + 3 = 4.
